A keepsake

Our First Three Months Together

From the day I met you in Siruvanur to our honeymoon in the hills of Kodaikanal — nineteen places, one new life together.

scroll to begin ↓

A New Beginning

It all started in Siruvanur, Thiruvallur, where I met you for the very first time. Whatever we said to each other that day must have mattered, because it carried us to the MRM Mandapam in Thiruvallur for our engagement — that quiet ceremony where promises became plans.

The months that followed moved fast. We passed through Annanur railway station, stopped at Illanirkolam Amma Kovil for blessings, and had our house warming in Kattupakkam — new walls, new beginnings. Then we returned to that same MRM Mandapam in Thiruvallur, this time for our wedding.

Marriage in our families begins with blessings, and ours did too — a trip up the hill to Thiruthani Murugan Temple to seek grace for the life ahead. Then came something quieter and just as important: my first day at your home in Kozhumanivakkam, the start of our everyday life together.

And then, the small joys that make a marriage feel real — our first movie together at Vijay Mall watching Kanna Laddu Thinna Aasaiya, a stop at the Fastrack watch shop in Anna Nagar, another movie night at Anna Nagar theatre for Vishwaroopam, dinner at Thalapakatti, an evening of shopping at Madarsha, an avocado juice in Anna Nagar, and quiet visits to Thirumullaivoyal Sivan Temple, Padi Sivan Temple, Kuthambakkam, and Poondhamalli Temple.

And tying it all together — our honeymoon in Kodaikanal, where the hills gave us space to just be a newly married couple, away from everyone else, before one last stop together in Perambur.

Ninety days. Nineteen places. One whole new life we began together.

A note, just for us

Our Love Note

If I could write to you across these ninety days, I'd say this: thank you for every small moment that became a memory — my nervous first hello, the quiet temple mornings, the silly arguments over which movie to watch, the avocado juice we shared in Anna Nagar.

None of these places would mean anything to me without you standing beside me in them. Here's to all the ordinary days still ahead, which I already know will become extraordinary — simply because I get to spend them with you.

— Your Deepika
